name: Current Affairs in TN History/Culture/Heritage (11): National Handicrafts Award for TN's Stone Carving Tradition

sourceRefs.label: newsonair.gov.in - National Handicrafts Awards 2023 & 2024 ceremony (9 December 2025) — T Baskaran of Tamil Nadu honoured for stone carving

researchNotes: Thirteenth and final TN-Culture-unit current-affairs topic of this 11th lap, completing Part A's 11th full rotation. Connects to Tamil Nadu's long stone-sculpture tradition (Mahabalipuram/Pallava-era stone carving heritage) via a concrete, named, dated 2025-26 award.

reading.intro: Covers the National Handicrafts Awards ceremony (December 2025) and Tamil Nadu's recognition in the stone carving category.

qa.question: When was the National Handicrafts Awards ceremony held, and which years' awards did it cover?
qa.answer: 9 December 2025, in New Delhi — conferring National Handicrafts Awards for BOTH 2023 and 2024 (a combined ceremony covering 2 years).

qa.question: Who presented the awards, and who from Tamil Nadu was honoured, in which category?
qa.answer: President Droupadi Murmu presented the awards. T. Baskaran of Tamil Nadu received the National Award for 2024 in the STONE CARVING category.

qa.question: Why is a stone carving award particularly significant for Tamil Nadu?
qa.answer: Tamil Nadu has a long, renowned stone-sculpture tradition, most famously associated with the Pallava-era monuments at Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram) — this award recognises a living artisan continuing that ancient craft tradition.

qa.question: Name 2 other artisans (from other states) honoured at the same ceremony.
qa.answer: Rupban Chitrakar (West Bengal, painting category) and Baldev Baghmare (Madhya Pradesh, tribal artisans category).

questions.prompt: The National Handicrafts Awards (for 2023 and 2024) ceremony was held on:
T. Baskaran of Tamil Nadu received the National Handicrafts Award 2024 in which category?
Who presented the National Handicrafts Awards at this ceremony?
Tamil Nadu's stone carving tradition (honoured by this award) is most famously associated with which historical monuments?
